URL: https://www.numberanalytics.com/blog/ultimate-guide-migration-research-methods

SUMMARY:
The main research methods used in migration studies include qualitative, quantitative, and mixed methods approaches. Qualitative methods involve in-depth interviews, focus groups, and ethnographic research, while quantitative methods include survey research, statistical analysis, and big data analysis.

--- SOURCE 4: Mixed methods: bridging the qualitative–quantitative divide in ... ---
URL: https://www.prio.org/publications/14130

SUMMARY:

FULL CONTENT:
![Peace Research Institute Oslo (PRIO)](/img/Prio-logo-negative.svg)

# Mixed methods: bridging the qualitative–quantitative divide in migration research

**Book chapter**

Carling, Jørgen (2024) Mixed methods: bridging the qualitative–quantitative divide in migration research, in *How to Do Migration Research*. Cheltenham: Edward Elgar (33–40). DOI: <https://doi.org/10.4337/9781035306855.00011>.

This chapter argues that migration research is often strengthened by an integration of data and methods from both sides of the qualitative–quantitative divide. Such integration does not require a fully mixed-methods research design; also studies where one methodological approach dominates can benefit from the targeted application of other data and methods on a smaller scale. Recent developments in the research environment strengthen the opportunities for mixed-methods approaches. First, the shift in research funding towards large collaborative projects offers greater opportunities for coordinated collection and analysis of diverse data. Second, the promotion of open science makes more data available for secondary analyses, also in smaller projects such as a doctoral dissertation. In this chapter, I present a series of specific combinations of quantitative-dominated, qualitative-dominated, and balanced combinations. By demonstrating how methods can be mixed in practice, I aim to lower the threshold for doing so in migration research.

![How to do migration research](https://cdn.cloud.prio.org/images/933c1fc6303449329023beb656b1e576.jpg?x=600&y=1200&m=Scale&hp=Center&vp=Center&ho=0&vo=0&)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Qualitative research emphasizes understanding human experiences through interviews and observations, while quantitative research focuses on numerical data and statistical analysis to identify patterns in migration.

Data collection methods in migration studies include surveys for quantitative data and in-depth interviews or ethnographic studies for qualitative insights, enhancing the understanding of migration dynamics.

Sampling techniques vary; qualitative studies often use purposive sampling to gather diverse perspectives, while quantitative research typically employs random sampling to ensure representativeness in data.
